about.metaDescription: Dragon Games — a TCG and board-games club and shop in Novi Sad since 1994. WPN and OTS certified.
about.title: Where the gaming community comes together
about.lead: Dragon Games is a TCG and board-games club and shop in Novi Sad. Since 1994 we've brought players together, run tournaments and shared a love of card and board games.

story.title: Our story
story.p1: We started back in 1994 as a small group of enthusiasts gathered around Magic: The Gathering. Over the decades we've grown into one of the oldest and best-known spots for card and board game fans in Serbia.
story.p2: Today Dragon Games offers a wide range of products, regular tournaments and a space where generations of players still gather, learn and compete.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.p1: We believe games bring people together. Our goal is an open, welcoming community where everyone — from beginners to seasoned competitors — finds their place at the table.
mission.p2: We work to bring the latest releases, run fair and well-organized tournaments, and support the local card and board gaming scene.

wpn.title: WPN store
wpn.body: Officially certified Wizards Play Network store for Magic: The Gathering.

ots.title: OTS center
ots.body: Official Tournament Store for Yu-Gi-Oh! — sanctioned tournaments and prizing.

community.title: Community-driven
community.body: Three decades of bringing together players of all ages and skill levels.

events.title: Regular events
events.body: Weekly tournaments, prerelease events and meetups throughout the year.

cta.title: Drop by and meet us
cta.subtitle: Our doors are open to all game lovers. Visit us in Novi Sad or get in touch.

games.title: Games we nurture
games.subtitle: From Magic to Riftbound — every game has its place at the table here.

guides.metaDescription: Beginner guides: Magic, Pokémon, Yu-Gi-Oh! and Riftbound — how to start, the formats we host, and FAQs.
guides.title: Game guides
guides.intro: New to trading card games? Pick a game and learn how to start, which sets we recommend, and which formats we host at the Dragon Games club.
guides.startersEmpty: Recommendations coming soon — drop by the shop for advice.

mtg.tagline: The deepest trading card game in the world.
mtg.overview: Magic: The Gathering is a strategy game where you play a powerful wizard and use a deck of cards — spells, creatures and lands — to defeat your opponent. Easy to learn, with nearly endless depth.
steps: Grab a Starter or Foundations set to learn the basics.
Pick two mana colors you like and build a 60-card deck.
Come to FNM (Friday Night Magic) and play your first matches with our players.
Grow your collection through booster packs and new sets.

faq.q: Do I need an expensive deck to start?
faq.a: No. Foundations and Starter sets are affordable and contain everything you need for your first games.

faq.q: How many cards are in a deck?
faq.a: A constructed deck has a minimum of 60 cards; a Commander deck has exactly 100.
Yes — FNM nights are relaxed and ideal for learning alongside experienced players.

pokemon.tagline: Catch them all — on the field and in your deck.
pokemon.overview: Pokémon TCG is a dynamic game where you use Pokémon, Trainer cards and Energy to knock out your opponent's Pokémon. Colorful, approachable and fun for all ages.
steps: Start with an Elite Trainer Box or a ready-made deck.
Learn the energy types and how to evolve your Pokémon.
Practice against friends or come to our Pokémon matches.
Upgrade your deck through new sets and single cards.

faq.q: What age is the game for?
faq.a: Pokémon TCG is great for kids and adults — the rules are simple, the strategy deep enough.

faq.q: What is an Elite Trainer Box?
faq.a: A set with boosters, energy and accessories — the best start for new players.

faq.q: Can I play without a big collection?
faq.a: Yes, ready-made decks are great to start and are ready to play out of the box.

yugioh.overview: Yu-Gi-Oh! is a fast game of summoning powerful monsters and chaining effects. Known for its combos and dynamic matches, it rewards creativity and card knowledge.
steps: Pick up a Structure Deck — a ready-made deck designed for learning.
Learn the core card types: monsters, spells and traps.
Play your first duels with help from our OTS players.
Upgrade your deck with new sets and the current meta.

faq.a: Official Tournament Store — the status to run sanctioned Yu-Gi-Oh! tournaments with prizing.

faq.q: Is the game hard to start?
faq.a: Structure Decks are made exactly for beginners and are easy to learn.
Duels are fast — usually 10–20 minutes per match.

riftbound.tagline: The official trading card game of League of Legends.
riftbound.overview: Riftbound is a new card game set in the League of Legends universe. Play your favorite champions, control the battlefield and outplay your opponent in fast, tactical matches.
steps: Start with a Starter deck focused on a single champion.
Learn how to fight for battlefields and use resources.
Come to our Riftbound matches and meet the community.
Grow your collection through new releases.

faq.q: Do I have to play League of Legends?
faq.a: No — Riftbound is a standalone game, though familiar champions are a nice bonus.

faq.q: Where can I try the game?
faq.a: We host demo nights where you can play without buying anything.

faq.q: Is the game available at your shop?
faq.a: Yes, we bring in Riftbound products as soon as they become available.
